summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #694 for the ars-ui repository, specifically fixing edge cases in RatingGroup and TagGroup components around pending controlled state, focus preservation, and aria attribute correctness.
summary.outcome: All four initial Codex threads were resolved with commit 288dc6fa, and five additional threads were addressed in a follow-up commit covering pending-state regression tests, disabled focus preservation, and duplicate code cleanup. Full coverage remained above the 90% branch threshold.

repo: The project uses cargo xtask for CI tasks: `cargo xtask spec validate` for spec validation and `cargo xtask ci coverage` for the full coverage pipeline
Coverage is reported as a merged native+wasm result and has a per-crate branch threshold (ars-components minimum is 90%)
cargo insta is used for snapshot testing; snapshots must be reviewed and reapplied when component output changes
The project uses a `waiting-for-codex-review` agent skill defined in `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` that governs the review loop protocol
Codex review is re-triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment after resolving threads
RatingGroup uses `data-ars-fraction` for partial fills and `data-ars-half` reserved for exactly half fills
TagGroup connect API exposes post-removal item collection; attrs for removed/absent tags return hidden non-gridcell output
RatingGroup labels must not emit a `for` attribute pointing at non-labelable controls; aria-labelledby is used instead

code.finding: RatingGroup pending controlled state and explicit whole-step builder logic; step(1.0) rounds and renders as whole-number radio behavior after allow_half(true)
TagGroup on_props_changed, Event::SelectTag handling, and disabled focus preservation logic; a duplicate block was accidentally introduced during patching and had to be manually cleaned up
Tag removal handling and hidden aria attribute emission for absent tags

workflow: When applying multiple sequential patches to the same file, verify with `nl -ba` line inspection between patches to avoid duplicate block insertion

The PR review loop requires: write test first for each finding, verify coverage doesn't regress, push, reply to each thread with the fixing commit hash, resolve threads via GraphQL mutation, then post @codex review
gh api GraphQL mutations are used directly to resolve review threads: `resolveReviewThread` mutation with thread ID
Thread IDs for resolution must be fetched separately from comment database IDs used for replies

friction: A patching mistake introduced a duplicate TagGroup block in tag_group/mod.rs, requiring manual inspection with `nl -ba` and a cleanup patch before continuing
Five additional unresolved Codex threads were discovered after the initial four were resolved, extending the session significantly
The full `cargo xtask ci coverage` pipeline is slow due to rebuilding instrumented workspace and running both native and wasm coverage suites
Codex review state polling required multiple manual gh API calls since there's no direct hook for when Codex finishes re-reviewing
open_items: One duplicate explicit-step Codex thread was noted as already covered by 288dc6fa but was not explicitly confirmed resolved in the transcript — worth verifying all threads are closed
The five additional Codex threads found mid-session may have introduced further findings upon the re-triggered @codex review that were not yet addressed in this session
